commands.review_summary:
Adds one Claude OAuth lifecycle test that stubs the refresh-token exchange, verifies CodexBar-owned cache writes, and checks the refreshed cache is treated as Claude CLI-owned once CLI keychain storage appears.

Reproducibility: Do we have a high-confidence way to reproduce the issue? Not applicable for this test-only PR; the added coverage exercises mocked OAuth refresh and keychain ownership seams rather than a reported failing runtime path.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Needs contributor/maintainer handling for draft readiness and real behavior proof; there is no narrow automated code repair to queue.

target.title: Prove Claude OAuth ownership handoff
target.body:
Summary:
- Add a focused lifecycle test for CodexBar-owned Claude OAuth refresh.
- Assert the refresh-token grant request, refreshed token decode, and CodexBar-owned cache write.
- Prove that once Claude CLI keychain storage is present, the fresh cached record resolves as Claude CLI-owned without rewriting production behavior.

Why:
- Refs #1287. The remaining OAuth lifecycle question is refresh-token ownership: CodexBar may refresh only credentials it owns, and Claude CLI storage takes ownership when present.
- This is test-only proof for the ownership/storage boundary, intended to complement the cookie renewal and cookie cache isolation PRs.

Proof:
- `./Scripts/lint.sh format` -> `0/1099 files formatted.`
- `swift test --filter ClaudeOAuthCredentialsStoreCLIStorageOwnershipTests` -> `Test run with 5 tests in 1 suite passed.`
- `swift test --filter ClaudeOAuthCredentialsStoreTests` -> `Test run with 17 tests in 1 suite passed.`
- `make check` -> `Done linting! Found 0 violations, 0 serious in 1098 files.`
